top_signals.notes: Best active signal. Consistent across horizons. FOMC/CPI proximity correctly drives risk-off.
Best LLM signal. Low activation (2.2% XAG) but high quality.
MUCH better at 3h. Day-of-week effects real short-term.
Best at 3h (74.2%) but terrible at 1d. Correctly horizon-gated.

worst_signals.notes: ACTIVELY HARMFUL. 12pp worse than random. Must be force-HOLD.
Consistently bad. Only 5.3% activation on XAG.
Bad with massive sample. WORSE at 3h. 92-100% correlated with ema/macro_regime.
At threshold. 92% correlated with trend. Redundant.

correlation_clusters.name: Trend Followers
correlation_clusters.issue: 8 signals = 1 independent viewpoint. Trend following overweighted 8x in consensus.

correlation_clusters.name: Mean Reversion
correlation_clusters.issue: 4 signals nearly always agree. Low activation (10-15% on XAG).

correlation_clusters.name: Permanent BUY (BTC)
correlation_clusters.issue: CRITICAL: Always vote BUY on BTC. Zero predictive value. Add 4 permanent BUY votes creating systemic bullish bias.

correlation_clusters.name: Permanent SELL (BTC)
correlation_clusters.issue: Only fires SELL. Permanent bearish bias.

regime_performance.observation: Current regime: range-bound. Trend cluster (8 signals) performs worst in ranging markets, explaining mass accuracy degradation.

unidirectional_bias_problem.description: Multiple signals show >90% directional bias on specific tickers, meaning they always vote one direction regardless of market conditions.
unidirectional_bias_problem.impact: These 5 signals add a net +3 permanent BUY bias to BTC consensus. This is not prediction, it is noise.

recommendations.action: Add unidirectional bias gate: exclude signals >90% same-direction over 500+ samples
Implement correlation-cluster voting: group >85% correlated signals, 1 vote per cluster
Regime-conditional signal gating: different weights for trending vs ranging
